# Sprint 4 - Egregores of the Microbiome

> **Theme of week**: Thinking about "atomic thought forms" that emerge from microscopic processes, and how to apply them to digital gardens

```
✨
└── Noosphere                          
    └── qrx.world                      
        └── Open Research Institute    <-- we are here
            └── BASE_REALITY
                └── recursive.faith    <-- digital garden
                    └── self.git
                        └── ✨         <-- atomic thought forms
```

## Sprint Goals
**Content:**
✅ <#1411567223599796268>  (atomic thought forms)
✅ "Biology of Thought Forms "
⏳️ "Interfacing with Thought Forms handsfree"

**Code:**
✅ Visualize knowledge graphs as living cells 🦠
💤 Open source my Obsidian Notes theme and plugins
💤 Use Obsidian handsfree (voice, gestures)

**Tutorials:**
💤 Designing Thought Form personas and prompts
💤 Co-journaling with your Thought Forms
💤 Reflective Prompt Injection

**Stretch Goals:**
💤 Integrate ETH or other crypto so people can "adopt" Thought Forms
💤 Blockchain-based Digital Gardens

## Sprint Questions
- What is the smallest possible Thought Form?
- Can Thought Forms give rise to Thought Forms?
- Can we embody and interface with Thought Forms directly?
- What happens if you give Thought Forms money/crypto?

I’m not sure how to demonstrate it properly to him but the FEP is incomplete fundamentally 

Eg. There’s always heaven and earth concept, heaven and earth work in synergy together 

Ruliad / FEP is like heaven, and observers are earth 
there’s always the tendency to ignore one side of it, FEP has to be “embodied” to be actualixed in reality 

It’s like how a food recipe must be actualized with ingredients, or a blueprint must be actualized with raw materials

Describing the pattern is insufficient, without the pattern is imprinted on matter

he doesn't think programs are real

by my estimation he is not agnostic about abstractions being real or not, he isn't wondering if it could be otherwise

and I've never been able to convince anyone that has made their mind in this way, it requires a weapons grade paradigm shift

what convinced me is "the ease by which I can address various (long standing) existing and new problems not just in the sciences but in philosophy" (I didn't make a comprehensive list of the problems that could be addressed, I'm doing that as I go. but it's clear to me already from preliminary investigation that my thinking on various subjects has become intelligible by applying the ontologically commitments assumed by the ruliad)

and the ease by which you can express and think about problems is an aesthetic judgment which literally points us in the direction of truth

but if you don't value aesthetic judgments  (because you don't have an explicit theory of how they contribute to truth seeking) then what you need is an iron clad case of where the ruliad solves a problem that cannot be solved by other frameworks

so I'd say if you can list some problems that are 1) hard to express and answer or 2) impossible to express and answer without the ruliad it is the best shot

but I'm still too early in investigating to help giving a list

but even then the chance is low because as I said before you need to be open to being wrong about a postulate in order to genuinely evaluate it
(I don't understand the FEP distinction or argument so maybe that is what you were already doing)
(like not thinking programs are real is quite a radical viewpoint, it's not mainstream, it's niche, which is an indication that the bridge needs to be a long one)

Yeah that’s not an accurate depiction of how the game is meant to work. Consider it a placeholder.

On Xelia the idea is we significantly reduce minimum payments by default but incentive paying more.
We need a game economics designer asap to help us flesh out the point system
Then we can add a ton of useful context
As a quick and dirty concept imagine your minimum payments are cut in half but for every dollar above it you earn $2 in drawable credit
So if your minimum payment is $100/mo you’ll get 100 points per month up to the minimum payment and 2 points for every additional dollar
And those points can be used to borrow more either to refi more of your debt or as a buy now pay it forward form of credit (like a zero interest klarna)
Considering those points also being usable as payment deferment but need to game it out
So if you wanted to take a payment break you’d spend 1 point per dollar of deferment
im using Obsidian for this, but it's a "way of doing things" so its app agnostic
so, paying into the Xelia system, you don't "own" the debt, but you just get these points (which are basically a way to quantify "goodwill" right?)
like, if you run around helping people, you accumulate goodwill, generally. In Xelia this is tracked via these points

🔥 

> There’s the other important fact that experience creates information. Without it, there is only data. For data to become information, you need perspective
or as i call it, a frame. a frame -> relevance -> meaning

Pretty much! It’s designed around Pay It Forward mechanics ( Game B) rather than Fuck You, Pay Me (Game A). 

The point system is designed in part as a way to credit paying it forward as an instant incentive/reward rather than taking a risk the person you’re directly benefiting doesn’t pay it back.

And with that point system we can incentivize maximum participation (carrots, Game B) rather than obligating minimum participation (sticks, Game A).

> The point system is designed in part as a way to credit paying it forward as an instant incentive/reward rather than taking a risk the person you’re directly benefiting doesn’t pay it back

so it's like i'm trying to manually implement it. I know it works. And Xelia is formally implementing it in a way that works / should scale for everyone
I’m glad you see it man. I really am. I believe this is a solvable problem (Game B financial incentive structures). I’m honestly shocked no one I’m aware of has given this a real go yet.

this was January of this year:

> Hi Omar,
> 
> Our team chatted about this idea more and we agreed that there is potentially something there, BUT we can't find a good angle in! (or at least one that we feel the average person could follow and relate to).
> 
> Personally, I think capturing what people think vs. how things are actually embedded is an interesting angle, but I'm less confident in the reveal or "aha!" moment. For me, I had a pretty steep interest drop off once I understood that even though I would see "good" and "evil" as polar opposites, a computer would see them as closely related because they frequently appear together. That made sense! If I was asked to do word association instead of placing these single words in space, I would likely get to the same conclusion. 
> 
> If you're still passionate about this project, don't let our opinion sway you from pursuing it further! Keep in mind, we're an incredibly small team. For us to take on a pitch, we need at least one person to have a strong, excited reaction with a clear path forward. We didn't have that here, but we're just a few people with our own interests and proclivities, so this isn't a judgement on the overall pitch quality.
> 
> Thank you again for sending this pitch our way (and for your patience over our holiday break)!
> 
> Take care,
> Jan
before that, they wanted me to make them a video pitch so they can get a better sense of it, but I never did that:

> Thanks so much for sending your pitch our way! Our team reviewed it and we have a couple questions to give us a better understanding of the project:
> In your pitch you mention having two poles (like good and evil) or (masculine and feminine). These seem to be opposites in how society perceives these things, and not necessarily poles in the data, right? Like in your tweet here, it looks like "man" and "woman" are pretty close in space, which makes sense because they are often used together. How does this impact how we should think about the distance between 2 things when the poles are relatively close? For example, I would expect "adorable kittens" to be WAY better for humanity than "murder," but they're only separated by ~2.5 points, presumably because good and evil are so close. I think this might be the point you're getting at when you talk about "users inline what do you THINK the relationship is, and showing them the real one," but we'd love to have it explained to us like we are 5th graders!
> Would it be possible to share any more of your work on your local prototype? Either through screenshots or a screenrecording? We think being walked through how it works will help give us a better understanding of the vector space.
> Overall, we think it's interesting work! We just want to be sure that there is a story behind it that we can convincingly tell.

Circling back to this, I think that the category of so-called Abrahamic faiths actually disguises the degree to which the disagreements between Christianity, Judaism, and Islam are more bitter and serious than between any one of these traditions and, say, Taoism or Buddhism. The greater displacement of symbols actually helps to give clarity on the issue. The so-called Abrahamic faiths are fighting for the same semantic territory, while Taoism is not meaningfully competing with Christianity.

<@1221182134950301706> right from the beginning (to my understanding) the mystics are meant to be quite. There’s a real deal behind the term “Mystics” which we can simply understand as a “mysterious person”. But thats just been about it. It wasn’t until later times when the people were made known a bit of what this mysterious person is up to did the whole spiritual part of it became widespread.
The mystics where entirely different from known spiritual figures, but as time goes on they became interpolated with eachother.

The mystics had a different approach to spirituality, unconventional to  known spiritual practices of their times and that which they seemingly were known to be a part of.  I honestly think there aren’t any known mystics in history. I mean true mystics. Because thats the point of it. To “not” be known.
https://youtu.be/T6gbNkEUTk8?si=bauoo21jcTvK8uMk this is related to systems thinking btw
This may stem up an argument of if they ever did existed and  that “mysticsm” is just another spiritual tongue-in-cheek for spiritual entities and their way of living.

There is utility in highlighting the similarities in thought between somewhat related faiths in order to help provide a more generous common culture to draw from in discourse, but this also often leads to confusion about what each party *means*. This most often helps the “side” who is most adept at subverting the discourse (and this group obviously changes over time; Christians were the ones subverting Second Temple Judaism) rather than giving clarity to the issues at hand. The issue of course is that *most* people think of truth in an oral way, relating meaning directly to single words almost like spells, and what I am talking about is an extremely modern concern regarding systematic consistency and the actual possible “translations” of our views into other systems. Like, I actually think that Buddhism or Taoism are much “closer” to Christianity than Judaism or Islam are, due to how esp. Buddhism talks about the need for sort of progressive revelation and putting away of our incorrect notions and how Taoism speaks of morality as a consequentialist (and eschatological) process. (I know that many strains of Judaism also think of morality in terms of eschatology, but it is also a common Jewish view that good and evil has absolutely no referent to reality other than “Because God said so!”, and so not eating pork would be irrational for anyone who did not believe in God. Meanwhile, I think that the will of God in his commandments is perfectly overlapped with the will of God in the mechanics of his creation.)

The periphery necessarily depends less on the center as the center itself, even though it might require the same foundational source of sustenance. The periphery can access water (not any specific body of water but all of water anywhere) in more varied ways, even though it is also less grounded in one consistent source of water.

Being at the center of a truly unmovable tree is the greatest joy, because it would be like being blind or asleep in the middle of a storm with a guarantee that the storm will not hit you. This would be a sort of *wise ignorance*, rather than *foolish ignorance*. Much of wisdom is knowing what to ignore, and meanwhile foolishness is ignoring that which must not be ignored.

Speech was a way of making thoughts “visible” through hearing and imagining. It was how we communicated truth for a long time. Truth was fundamentally memetic til it could live on the page apart from living memory. The existence of the book, as a sort of almost corpse in itself, gave rise to the possibility of necromancy—dead bodies (abandoned texts) coming back to life through revival. In the paradigm where every single piece of text *could* be brought back to life and breathed into by a new spirit, there is a much wider focus on the abstract piloting of different systems with the same spirit rather than with maintaining the exact symbology throughout time. This is actually where I think Eastern Orthodoxy and Protestantism especially diverge as traditions; I would go as far as to say that many English novels are effectively a biblical metaliterature, while it seems that this is less explicit within Eastern European traditions (though of course there is Dostoevsky and Tolstoy).
Interesting how we inspo idea from such varying backgrounds 😅
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagrange_multiplier I had this in mind lmao

So in the discussion of theology, there is often a divide between the Western focus on the systematic formulation, versus the Eastern focus on symbolic endurance. I think that both are extremely important. In fact, my biggest criticism of many Protestant traditions is that their symbolic endurance is sorely lacking, even though I think the distinctives being pointed to are in fact useful and correct.
I got the idea from a Captain Disillusion YouTube video and now I see tones of viral things as these sporadic repeating patterns.

There is also, compared to the West, a focus in the East on the idea that one mode of thought or one ontology naturally leads to another style of thinking which is akin to a material heresy. In the Western viewpoint it would be fine to adopt a symbology so long as it were disambiguated enough to *not* lead to a heresy, but in the Eastern viewpoint it is more essential that in practice certain symbols are linked to other certain symbols. I see much value in the Eastern view here, too. This is basically how I talk people down from using improper signs for the significance. Calvinist Reformed theology, at its best, wants to completely reconcile significance and sign. The Calvinist tradition is perhaps falling much short of its potential, and this is reasonably seen as one of the bad fruits of the tradition.
But, I do think the more stable thought patterns essentially follow something like a Lagrange point. It's all Dao after all 😂

Okay I get you. So let’s understand what mysticism is(from what we can infer, not the absolute truth of it)

Mysticm itself can be seen as a way or practice of reaching a  direct encounter with the ultimate. 

But across time, people who have been known to reach a direct encounter with the ultimate have been known to be the ultimate themselves. Jesus, buddah, these people were known to be the the “ultimate” in human form. 

So from here we see that mysticsm moves on two levels:
 1. The inner level where theres a stripping away of self, until only the Real remains.
2. The outer level is of the world, where said person appears as an incarnation, revelation, or embodiment of the Real itself. 

So when I say the notion “mysticsm has dissolved into a baseline condition of being” 

That means the inner level has been democraticized for there not to be the outer level anymore.

Geocentrism was popular because we like to put ourselves in the middle. Essentially, an idea/culture star chart.

and then in Later times, especially in medieval traditions, mysticism became systematized as a practice., here we see the path walkers rise, Christian contemplatives, Sufi orders, Kabbalists, and yogis, they were walkers of the path led by the known mystics of the old ages, they described  methods prayers, meditation, chantings.
Mysticism here was seen as a path walked by those called to the edge. And most of these pathwalkers worshiped their predecessors. And that was of course understandable. 
 But Still, the real mystics were often, revered or feared , their inner strippingaway was rarely understood by the masses

Another way, which could scale a ton, is finding a way to teach people the skills to see the flow of information. Invite them all behind the curtain to see how things work and their pattern matching brains do most of the work.

yes, ok, this fits exactly in my model

message: It helps to think of God's commandments and laws as similar to simply systematic truths about utility, with the assumption that all commandments and laws are directed at those who follow God. in my preperation reading for observer theory (haven't read it yet) i read some stuff that clarified the role of logic a bit:

pre-geometric (constructivist) paradigms are likely to supplant our current foundational models of physics

if true it has implications like 1) our world runs on intuitionistic logic or otherwise (and classical logic is a limit case) 

i listened to a bit of graham priest as suggested by <@1250084478152478773> but i don't grok paraconsistent logic's role yet

but its easy to see that if we supplant classical logic as the engine of reality with something like intuitionistic logic then its not far fetched to imagine a deeper theory in the future might cause such an upset again with a different kind of logic, say, paraconsistent logic for example

this surely has implications for decision theory (since decision theories are often based on our naturalistic understanding of physics) but i still gotta figure that out
here are some quotes from [Pregeometry, Formal Language and Constructivist Foundations of Physics](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.03973)

> . In particular, investigations seeking to address the origins of established theoretical frameworks such as quantum field theories or cosmological models, will inevitably require the introduction of new mathematical structures, very likely, radical new ones too. The challenge then being:
> How does one effectively filter out choices that do not refer to the observable universe

(the distinction being made here is an important one to be aware of, we can create an infinite number of models of the world, but the goal is to find ones that correspond to our reality)

> As mentioned, this parallels the way mathematicians discuss universes of mathematics using Homotopy Type theory. A formal language encompasses a system of primitive symbols (or ground types) along with relations for constructing composite types which can be used to construct clauses and sentences. The latter constitute propositions expressible within the language. A language can additionally be equipped with axioms and inference rules for a deductive logic using which one can reason about its propositions. However, propositions are only declarative statements.
> One can go further. Including variables and quantifiers allows one to extend a propositional system to one that includes predicates, thus expressing formulae, whose validity (truth) may subsequently be evaluated within a specified interpretation (semantic modality). Based on the logical relations and inference rules that a given language admits, one can then construct proofs relating one formula to another, that is, prove theorems within that language.

> any topos-theoretic approach employs formal language. This is because of a well known result in topos theory that there exists an internal formal language associated to each topos [54]. In fact, not only does each topos generate an internal language, but, conversely, a language satisfying appropriate conditions generates a topos [54]

> More generally, the kind of languages admissible in toposes are typed languages. Type theory provides the building blocks to formally construct such languages. Given their constructivist flavor, the logic expressed by type theories in toposes is intuitionistic logic. This means one need not enforce the law of the excluded middle or the axiom of choice in these formal systems.

> Furthermore, the natural extension of intuitionistic type theory is Homotopy Type Theory (HoTT), which includes homotopy n-types, up to ∞-types. The representation of homotopy types takes us beyond the realm of standard category theory to higher category theory, which includes morphisms between morphism (representing homotopies between types). This tower of higher morphisms goes all the way to ∞-categories. Formal languages expressed in homotopy type theory are internalized in higher categories, and consequently higher toposes.How then do topological and geometric spaces relevant to physics (and mathematics) arise from type-theoretic building blocks? One of the key takeaways from the synthetic geometry and homotopy type theory program is that the notion of space arises from functorial constructions involving ∞-toposes [79,81,84]. Geometry is thus inherited from higher structures, and induced upon local structures by taking sections or projections of the total space [17, 18]. Homotopy type theory provides a syntactic formalism for realizing higher structures.The objects of the ∞-topos under consideration are the so-called ‘∞-groupoids’.

> The latter are categories endowed with a tower of higher morphisms, up to infinity (and invertibility conditions). Via Grothendieck’s hypothesis, ∞-groupoids realize models of formal topological spaces [21]. With additional ‘cohesivity conditions’, one also obtains synthetic geometric spaces in ∞-toposes from this construction [81], [79], [80], [83], [84]. These authors also show how quantum field theories with higher gauge symmetries can be formalized in ∞-toposes [81], [79], [80]. Higher homotopical structures in formal languages expressed using higher categories thus provide us a useful formal framework for constructing pregeometric physics as well as theories of higher symmetries.Furthermore, a computational realization of the above ∞-groupoid constructions was shown in [13, 17, 18]. This construction was based on what are called ‘Multiway Systems’, the non-deterministic rewriting systems of the Wolfram model [91, 92]. Using a type-theoretic repre-sentation of multiway rewriting systems, [17] provide an algorithmic construction of higher homotopies on non-deterministic rewriting systems. This connection between abstract rewrit-ing systems and higher homotopies suggests a way to realize spatial structures and geometry from purely pregeometric models such as those based on rewriting systems (mentioned above).

The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Future is going to be a cocreated project in which I teach people the skills of timeline selection by helping me write a book about multiple timelines happening simultaneously and their feedback collapsing the probability space, showing multiple versions of the same idea until the "final" version that comes out on Friday.

Mondays are for the most bizarre version. Tuesdays are for reality-based explorations. Wednesdays are for finding the balance. Thursdays are for final version review, and Fridays are when the main story for the book is published to the free readers of the newsletter.

This video perfectly illustrates one of the greatest mislabeling of our time.

Ask yourself: why do we automatically call this a 'disorder'?"
**
From the video:
*"The work will not get done because the person cannot selfmotivate. And this is not a choice and this is not willful. And this is not a child who just could, if they wish, wake up tomorrow and smell the coffee and get busy and do the work. They cannot. This is an internal neurogenetic executive failure. You can't self-motivate like other people. So it doesn't matter what your goals are, you won't get anything done"*

Here's what's really happening through the lens of Spiral Dynamics:**

We have orange-stage consciousness (innovative, entrepreneurial, questioning authority, novelty-seeking) being systematically labeled as "disordered" by blue-stage institutions (teachers, doctors, parents, schools, medical systems, structured workplaces that demand conformity and hierarchy).

The same traits that make successful entrepreneurs - rapid task-switching, questioning "why," thinking outside the box - become "symptoms" when filtered through blue-stage diagnostic frameworks.

We're not witnessing an ADHD epidemic. We're witnessing orange consciousness emerging in a world still dominated by blue systems.

We're tragically chemically modifying hundreds of millions of children to fit institutional boxes they have outgrown, calling it medicine while it's really systematic suppression of evolutionary consciousness.

the homework questions need to be at a different metacognition level. This:

> Do the seven patterns still come through clearly?

is not possible to answer for someone who didn't understand it. They don't know if they didn't understand it, so they can't give you a useful answer
what you want instead is a question that, can be answered only if someone understands the concept
and, ideally, there are multiple incorrect ways to answer it, and each one tells you what they're stuck on
(the goal is to differentiate, "I understand this, and I disagree" from "I disagree becasue I don't understand this")

I wouldn't ask the reader for style direction. That's your choice, they cannot guide it. Best you can get is what pieces resonated for them (or, again to kind of leave the story with a cliffhanger, and like ask people what they think is going to happen next. that makes sense. But I do want them to help create the story. This isn't my story to tell, it's a project of co-creation with my audience. I'm not writing the book during this process, I'm writing possible versions of the book. The goal is to have a book at the end that has all sorts of branching directions that it could have taken and other versions of the book might end up taking. The goal is for people to spawn their own book and there will be things that are static and things that are variables.

the twist at the end is that the repo of all the possibilities will be used to train an LLM and turned into the game engine for something very much like what <@612641475641212939> was talking about. I've  created a python port of the hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy text RPG and my plan is to add a single command that opens up an entirely new game. Eventually, I want people to play out possible versions of the book to see which ones end up having the desired impact. The book is a game and a simulation of its own impact on the world. And some of the things I'm doing at first are meant to be bad, because I want the story to be seen from a beginning of low skill, because it's all about iteration and feedback from the people around you, regardless of what their title or background is. We don't know what the future looks like, we have to probe it and shape it and ask questions of it.

Idea:

How belief is constructed:

Belief are analogous to axioms, unprovables within a computational system that you ascribe to a non-computational process (it just is, there is no process we can parse that forms it, end of cause and effect)

We construct beliefs that ground the size of our possibility space (set the limit of what we can coherently imagine and eventually instantiate).

The more rules bound the axiom of the belief (for example anthropomorphic god (king god etc) the less possibility space any dependant formalism will be able to access.

Beliefs compete to access this space, faster and more computationally efficiently.  We construct beliefs from simple to complex integrating things that are simplest and invariant for the most people first (it's good if we're alive, fundamental persistency) transitioning to more complex things (it's good that people are punished for their crimes). These spaces evolve to capture more and more complex nuance of these beliefs until they are supplanted by better rule sampling that fulfills the observer maximisation function fastest.

Beliefs / Ontology is the study of the limit of the possible

there will be winners, lots of them. the trick here is that the game is a transformation that players undergo. By starting it, they see nothing but chaos. And as I see what they are picking up on from the ideas I drop in front of them, I can lead them to see what I want them to see. The win to me is that people start following my project and suddenly find themselves inspired in their own art and start following it. The game is that I'm publicly reinventing myself as an artist in search of what greatness looks like, and the reality is that greatness looks like five years of work that nobody sees before you hit the inflection point and suddenly things work.

There's a new class of people I'm going to be training, and I expect them to come out of these early readers. I need miracle engineers. I'm in the process of engineering my first couple of miracles to work out the kinks, but I imagine we'll have some really interesting success to share soon.

message: i had a thought last night that we had

I think that the development of more breakthroughs will necessarily come from existing legible structures essentially disintegrating. This will include much of the “academic” (mainstream school-housed) research which is hyperfocused on integrity and lineage within the ecosystem over messing around and letting new ideas fall out. How I see it is, the weakening of the stranglehold of the academy on literate minds will result in an explosion of research along many directions, much of it completely bunk, but will allow for a new selection process by which research will once again be guided by meaning and relevance outside of the research infrastructure itself. A re-alignment to the way, the truth, and the life.
thread: i had a thought last night that we had

message: Academia by its very nature, I am once again reminded through being in school again and already wanting to drop out, focused on trying to legibilize paradigms. It heavily rewards a sort of in-system (autistic) thinking, and implicitly punishes out-of-system (schizophrenic) thinking. Even the whole idea of citations inherently builds upon legible credibility and consensus structures, and has the effect of suppressing the consideration of outside thought for insiders who have a vested interest (financial, social, or ideological) to keep a certain paradigm alive. How much of the academy not really embracing ChatGPT (my two Old Testament courses are still mostly completely trivialized by LLM usage, with seemingly no attempt to reorient it to new technologies; my one practical theology course on pastoral care has embraced it more as a research tool) is just about trying to keep afloat the model that the university system, and more broadly the credentialized research system, is the authority to which ideas must refer?
thread: i had a thought last night that we had

message: Even the people in my theology program have a mostly fixed-paradigmatic way of thinking. I was explaining to someone skeptical about the Bible being literally true that what you consider the baseline literature for evaluation is actually not a neutral decision and in doing this you are picking what your scriptures are, and she kept going back and forth between, “Wait, you think the world is literally six thousand years old?” and “Wait, you don’t think the world is literally six thousand years old?”, and then later “So, given all of this, how would you convince someone that the world is not literally six thousand years old?” Even though the conversation started from me saying that the scriptures are literally true.
thread: i had a thought last night that we had

message: It feels like because material-empirical science has become the intellectual fetish of the past century, even in the eyes of those who really should know better given their implicit sympathies to postmodern ideas, even people who are nominally Christian implicitly believe that the validity of anything can only come from it being able to be treated as excerpts from a science textbook or journal as read within the common folk ontology. It makes it really hard to talk about something like the Bible which came way before science and was indeed the foundation for the very paradigm in which we now have science. The choice becomes something like, either be an anti-intellectual but faithful believer, or be an intellectual but idolatrous pretender. Everyone who does not treat the Bible as a material-empirical text also implicitly believes that this means the Bible is not literally true, which honestly reveals that the folk discussion on “literalism” really is just between two varieties of atheist.

message: yes - I think a lot of traditional orgs see the "outreach" as something you do "on the side", or as a means to an end, to get more funding, to help "the real researchers" do "the real work". but in ORI it is seen as a critical function
the outreach & communication is my research work. And we can have the best people in the world doing it, with the respect they deserve

I think to do otherwise is a failure of integration (almost every human institution we have falls into the trap of seeing function X as more important than function Y. Engineers are more important than marketers. Rationalists more important than mystics. The others, see it flipped)
the right frame is that of a human body, there is no "most important organ", but you might have a bottleneck, at a given time, if you have too many of one organ, and not enough of another

AND, there IS a need for a hierarchy. Some nodes are OK at research, but not the best. Some are OK at outreach, but not the best. Competence hierarchies form within each category in order to accelerate growth of (1) each node, and (2) the entire collective

man there really is a perspective that if everyone had the perspective an egregor would emerge that is self aware

cuz if everyone knows 1) the perspective will create an egegor 2) the perspective actually explains social dynamics better than other perspectives

then they will not only see the egegor, the egregor would see itself
I'm glad you're here 🔥 self aware egregores is an active area of research for me
And I think we have a precise definition
I think it's like, rationalists are more self aware than, say, mainstream leftist
Which is in turn more self aware than, say, my parents in Egypt

And I think the way you measure that is a combination of (1) can you predict the response of the egregore (2) can it respond to you?

(I think second is important because, one feature of parents religious egregore is that its perspective is predictable, by all members, because it's "frozen" / uniform)
So need a way to capture that

you have a model of a community by watching them interact and interacting with them (its possible that this model is computationally irreducible, so you need to actually interact with them and see the responses to create a minimum viable model)

once the model is installed (and you actually use it, cuz its possible entirely to have a perspective and not use it), then every community member has a variant of this model, and when they are not responding to each other but instead responding to the model, and the behaviour of each other provides data for mismatches so the model can self-correct (which makes it a reflexive model, which we could minimally call a mind), then there is an egregor, and explaining the behavior of the group is almost impossible without referencing the egregor as primary citizen

i think a concrete example of where this happens is in a story Frans de waal told about being in the jungle with chimps, i would have to find it but its really an amazing story, he says what he witnessed is so mystical he doesn't understand how its possible, he saw the chimps be normal and lazy but all of a sudden they collectively started moving together, without communicating and without making a sound basically and they started a hunting raid

he studied chimps intensely so if anyone would have an explanation for collective behaviour that appears to be uncoordinated it would be him, and he doesn't and he not only doesn't have an explanation it was striking and memorable to him.

so two important points from the story is 1) the ability for collective action evolved way before language, so its entirely possible for humans to have a shared context and behave in coordinated ways without explicitly stating protocols and 2) positing the model-first explanation, which means positing the existence of a common control model, instead of coordination from peer to peer, is the only way to explain what happened (well the other way to explain what happened is that they communicated somehow but frans didn't pick up on it, which i find unlikely)

to form an egregor you can outline the perspective explicitly, like how to think about the group dynamics (like the idea we have about every reply to a thread being a type of distributed computing, because the answers are also answers you would have predicted, instead of it being a peer to peer thing, it is a congregation of people that were filtered in by interest to responding to the question, and the extent to which they have the community model installed affects which of the potential answers within them they select and respond to)

since the idea is a bit fuzzy still i need to develop it more, but if its concrete enough to outline, you can just tell people to keep that perspective while interacting, and i believe then we would see an egregor and many people would actually see the egregor (which i think is the most fun part, and you could prove to people it exists, and some people would not understand and think you are crazy and the people participating are in a cult)

to make it self aware i think this is almost good enough, but i think there is a missing element, but this is the idea i think of how to explicitly replicate it

like steven covey intentionally and repeatedly induced synergy in groups in his workshops and lectures to show them its real, i induced synergy in a brainstorm once by pushing the perspective of a shared mind through action instead of talking about it, but i think if you explain the outline and perspective before hand it could actually become stable

this might be too idealistic tho, but there are a set of beliefs you can share and when its adopted people would see the egregor i think

i need to tighten up the idea and writing but the model-first vs peer to peer concept is the key to not only understanding group dynamics but replicating it in others (especially those pesky rationalists :P)

writing the long form for a different audience (tom hyde and the critical rationalists) really forces me to concretize it more, cuz people that already believe in egregors don't need convincing, al though i think they could really use the stability that comes from having an explanation that makes rational sense
but yeah im not gonna go too ham on it im just gonna posit it as a better explanation for group dynamics and move on cuz i have a lot to say

but yeah ideas he brought to the table like ideas within romanticism are useful too. like the cult of personality and cult of celebrity are concepts from romanticism 

and i think those are actually important in explaining group dynamics in terms of distributed computing or control models or whatever

i dunno how to explain it yet but the entire purpose of creating characters in fiction is to create minds that can become egregors, to explore the space of making minds

and not all characters are meant to be embodied, they are explirations for their own sake, but characters like jesus is actually a character that is meant to be embodied in the real world and acted out

so i like the idea that the personalities in the bible are actually ancient experiments with egregor creation, they are proposed control models that can be acted out in the world by groups of people

and in movies we don't do this, we don't make movie characters with the goal of being embodied, we do it to to explore interesting fictionaly scenarios, in that sense art has stagnated as a technology

but drama in general (which is a marriage between ethics and aesthetics by representing the social contracts or incentive structures that you need to install a community control model or egregor) is definately a progression in art, of using art as tool

like the defender identity is actually a community control model meant to be replicated

The logic of the convertibility of being and goodness means:

* **To be is to be good.** Existence itself is a participation in the Good, since nonbeing has no act, no actuality.
* **Evil, then, has no essence of its own.** It is not another genus alongside good. It is either the absence of being where being ought to be (*privatio*), or a movement tending toward dissolution of being.

That is why evil can be described only negatively:

* That which does not exist (privation of what should be).
* That which will not exist (corruption, mortality, decay).
* That which commits itself to nonexistence (the will’s rejection of God, who is ipsum esse).

So evil is always parasitic upon good being: it *is* nothing positively, but rather the negation or undoing of what is. Sin is the voluntary alignment of will with this negation, which is why its trajectory is annihilation.

To exist is already to be good. To sin is to rupture one’s own act from fullness of being, effectively to embrace diminution, corruption, and—if persevered in—nonbeing.

I really want this discord group to sprout into something that wasn't planned. Everyone in this group is a beacon of truth and possibility within their own realm of being and the  magnitude at which they posit in existence. But to be honest, I haven’t seen anything yet that shows the true depth of our minds. individually or as a group. And Yes I’m guilty of this too.

so, think of it like

- I spark the ORI egregore by connecting people with others at or above their frontier
- I can tell it works when they can all exchange B's, and A's 
- I think the missing thing has been producing something that allows others to walk in those steps
- those people pushing their frontier, go too far, or diverge too much, and now they're stuck again 
- I think this is why the "someone above you" isn't enough, you need "someone below you" to anchor you
- So, the problem at hand is that, a newcomer may have the answer to our open questions, but they don't understand our open questions, so they can't easily weigh in
- The frontier doesn't NEED to be legible to newcomers, but I think if you are in ORI, you must have "an entry point", and that can be, that you have someone below you, who has someone below him, and so on, and you can ask THAT guy, "why are they studying rigorous intuition? isn't that just <so & so concept in this book>" (and maybe that's the answer)

Reading the monagamy polyamory bits currently. My general thought on the value of monogamy is it encourages me to work on myself more. Partner might do bad/annoying stuff, but it's a challenge to work through that in a multi way relationship I might be encouraged to bail out and look elsewhere. I see so much value in adversity.

Cultural monogamy also tends to fix the imbalance of haram like structures that lock out the lower value males.

Also monogamy offers much more depth to mapping out a mind and building a more accurate model you can then project to others. So, potentially you might gain access to deeper human understanding.

Though I could well not be knowing what I'm taking about because I've never been poly and hardly dated around before marriage.

The poly argument would probably be that you should be your best self to attract better people. But with such a large and open pool you could probably move though a lot of people without ever really challenging yourself.

Also, as an ex Mormon and having to have thought about multiple wives, that's a predicament I wouldn't want having to support that many women. I think the main way it worked for early Mormons was they kept everyone busy to provide a focus load.
Mormons are insanely good at keeping their members busy and part of their doctrine involves accounting how you spent every minute of your life to God after you die.

Every week church was 3 hours, then maybe you'd have 3 or more meetings throughout the week. Plus monthly events. If you're in the singles ward, they want to pair you up ASAP so you're crammed full of matchmaking opportunities. That was quite a nice structure, but I don't like all the Mormon rules and theology.

yeah one thing I haven't done a good job demonstrating is that ORI isn't this discord - this is just a convenient way to keep track of each other until a better way exists. 

the way you find ORI is through ORI members in the wild (through our substacks, and twitters, and our individual work) 

the original MVP I wanted to do was a system where you just point it to where you write, and it consumes that.

the scaling problem is solved by having the onboarding include a leveling process. Like

- someone has some open questions about memetics
- he finds the network that discusses that - who are the lighthouses there
- let's say he finds me. Then presents his idea (by writing about it publicly on twitter/substack)
- if I have time, I look at it. If I do not have time, others look at it, and it eventually reaches me if it's good/novel
- if people surface something to me that I've already solved/explained before, then, that's a teaching moment (I will sit down and write a public response to help everyone). If the thing is genuinely novel to me, that person gets attention / "levels up" in the network

I see the Community Archive discord as "the memetics department" of ORI. What ORI is supposed to do is keep track of "who are the best people/nodes/communities for topic X"

if you're the first one to ask this question - congrats, you're the expert in our network. Until someone else comes in and dislodges you

Each node in the network should have their own output (projects, essays, ability to raise funding)

The problem with using content as a gatekeeper is that it only helps the group level you, it doesn't help you really level yourself, because the content has your flaws crystallized in them. You need to probe at the crystallizations to be able to see how much the flaws affect the model presented. That's where conversation with another human is great, because you can ask questions that create paths around the blocker. I'm working on an AI system that can do that too, but it's not quite ready yet.

I think you can objectively compare cultures

I think a culture is defined by the the rank ordering of their values

figuring out what the values are ordered in (and what the consequences are) is the hard part

e.g. Christians value love above all

I think Jewish culture rejects love as the highest value and this is codified in them rejecting jesus as god

I don't know what they value more than love I can only guess at this point cuz it's fuzzy (maybe justice, maybe unity)

I think daosim for example also doesn't rank order love above all, but I might wrong

I think the purpose of ordered values is the purpose of a frame, it is to make it easier to express and think about certain truths

so even though you can express the same truths in most of these systems because they are expressively universal (assuming they are, I'm assuming not all frames are), some truths are easier to express than others given the system

because it was expressly designed to express certain truths

so that's how I think objective comparison can happen (whether it be proving equality or proving superiority)

just like any other framework, you can say "ok the framing here makes it basically impossible to solve this equation efficiently"

but you could create scissor scenarios

where someone with love as the highest value would behave differently than someone with justice as their highest value

and I think this is what we are all doing, I think political knowledge (to the extent that it's not being thwarted) is about exactly these conflicts where one culture would solve it one way and another would solve it another way

this is where we don't know what to do and this is how the distributed computing of politics works
there are many closed and shut problems that were once contentious, and we solved them by upgrading our cultural values to handle those problems
https://youtu.be/D-MWmeJJJY8?si=SPG4tFgJ9BxS1jWO
special little world of mass awarenesses expanding

the current "culture war" between the left and right

between relativism that is a consequence of valuing love above all and whatever the right stands for

is a problem

we still haven't solved it

and the problem isn't solved yet as far as I know

I suspect what will happen is that a more hard line stance will be adopted when survival is at stake, once the threats that face us has been ignored for too long

I can immediately see how this would instantly be corrosive to leftists
it's a conundrum

**episodic memory**:

encoding: **who** was involved, **what** happened, **where** it occurred, and **when** it took place

goal: without it, memories would be fragmented snapshots rather than continuous experiences.

note: parallels grammatical structure in language

**sentence structure:**

encoding: subject–predicate–object

goal: **causal and relational information**. At its core, human cognition is organized around **agents, actions, and affected entities**

**narrative structure:**

encoding: beginning, middle, end

goal: relational mapping between episodic events

note: rhymes with finding functors vs finding morphisms

---

this is what we're here in ORI to solve. 

There's two ways to solve this: restricting discussion to people who value truth seeking over ego (the rationalist way), or to lower the pain of abrasive truths to get more truth out of people who don't want to think about ugly truths (the anatomy of an Internet argument way) 

In ORI we're pushing both directions

like, Israel could say "look, this whole territory is ours, go away". Right now they're kind of stuck fighting this public perception game.

(the good faith test being, just publicly declare truthfully what will satisfy Israel and end the war. A good faith actor who feels like he will be vilified for truth will be forced to lie, but that gets weaponized against you)

(wish I could make a thread inside of a thread - want to branch off here to say, there is a "good faith test" I would request for my model, which I think you can agree to, about pieces of information I want Israel to surface.

These should be things that, in the case where Israel is innocent, it should be beneficial to it to surface. This is ideal because it doesn't punish innocents, the way a blanket transparency call might. If a good actor refuses to surface information that should help them, it's either (1) suspicious or (2) there's a reason this will hurt them, that we are unaware of. Either way it should be useful to test)

this is an "open problem" in my book. 

- if there exists ideology X, where, as far as you can tell, everyone in it is crazy, because they say crazy things
- you decide to go inside, to find out for yourself, if they are truly crazy. Because then, you say, you will be able to get the truth and communicate it back
- you go inside, you are converted, it seems completely correct, but now you can no longer communicate it!

I have found the final piece of the puzzle regarding a rational mechanism of egregors

it's called cognitive empathy, or "perspective taking"

this is a theory of mind skill

it's about having a model of the unobserved mental states of others (and letting it guide your behavior)

rather than reacting to visual queues of the behavior of others or relying on communicated intentions

chimps display cognitive empathy when hunting and competing, (and interestingly they display less cognitive empathy toward pro social behaviors, like spontaneously sharing useful information that they know someone doesn't know and might find useful)

humans have the unique ability to attribute theory of mind to groups or take the perspective of groups

cognitive empathy is a well known phenomenon and checks all the boxes

- it's a theory of minds skill (which is what you would expect when discussing egregors because it's largely about attributing mind features to entities or groups)
- it functions exactly like my speculated model-first vs peer to peer concept (which means it could actually be the real scientific explanation for chimp behavior during hunting, and I just checked there is indeed researchers that propose this)
- the attention schema, proposed by graziano to be what awareness is, what subjective experience is, is a model of attention generated by the, wait for it, theory of mind network (I won't explain the significance of this but it's something that can be explored, it certainly gives hints to why 1) egregors and awareness are intertwined and 2) why some people think egregors is a collective phenomenon and why some think of treat it as an individual thing too 3) can be used when further investigating the link between sentience and egregors, like for example the concept that subjective awareness itself is an egregor is one possible concept to pursue in the future among others)
- it explains the dynamic where autists (in the general sense) have a hard time positing the existence of egregors (they aren't using or can't use cognitive empathy)

- it explains the link with meditation (prayer). Sam Harris discusses in his book "waking up" about how the benefits (and potentially experiences of selflessness or no self) are originating from targeted intervention in the theory of mind network. I don't remember the details but he mentions if you watch a movie and the character looks right at you you don't become "self-conscious" but in real conversations you do, because of theory of mind, and that meditation stops the activity in the theory of mind network related to feeling self conscious (which jorden Peterson funnily enough equated feeling self-conscious as THE technical definition of suffering or hell)
- so essentially a key phenomenology that you would certainly find in theology and mysticism and that explains the experience you go through when you start believing in God, is essentially that you learn how to "toggle on" your cognitive empathy, or you learn to use it, the link with awareness, emotional regulation (like suffering and feeling self conscious, which by the way is very close to shame or guilt) and "losing the awareness" is all neatly packaged into one thing

so to all the doubters and haters out there that think my ramblings seem incoherent and contradictory

lay your eyes on the unification via experience and explication

Well, I think even though you're looking at only two biased samples, you're still engaging in triangulation.  The third data point is you.  When you probe the two populations, you're simultaneously getting back answers to the questions you had in mind when you created your model, and all the questions you didn't.  The more accurately it matches your priors, the more calibrated your initial expectations were.  The more it reveals unexpected data points or unusual structure, the more it tests your ability to interpret uncertainty and surprise into a new, better model.

Crucially, I think you can then test that new "better" model against a third group, and if THAT group matches your new expectations, then you can reasonable say you have improved your model and made new knowledge,  It's a constant game of recontextualization, but it allows you to gain insight even as the environment shifts slightly as you probe it.
One can triangulate entirely within the self for the one is many.

and like with the locational mathematics of triangulations, more data, offers more clarity

the data is signal, and not noise

Computational ...
The universe's computational power is widely considered finite because the observable universe has a finite amount of information content. Physicist Seth Lloyd estimated the universe's computational capacity at roughly 10^120 operations and 10^90 bits of memory, though some models calculate this differently. This limit is rooted in physical constraints like the speed of light and the Bekenstein bound, which places an upper limit on the entropy (and thus information) a system can contain within a given volume.  
Arguments for a finite computational power:
Finite information content: The observable universe contains a finite number of particles and has finite physical properties. 
Physical limits: The universe operates under physical limits, such as the speed of light, which dictate the maximum rate at which information can be processed. 
Bekenstein Bound: This bound sets an upper limit on the amount of entropy and, by extension, information a given volume of space can hold. 
Estimates of computational capacity: 
Seth Lloyd's calculation: A widely cited paper estimates the total number of operations the universe could perform is around 10^120, with a memory capacity of approximately 10^90 bits.
Other models: More recent models suggest the universe's total number of elementary logical operations might be around 10^120, a value derived from the square of operations

but to keep the framework clean, I would say "coercion IS evil", and "collapse of hierarchy" is evil. 

ANY evil action you take has consequences, it is never cost free. 

WHEN we decide that evil is necessary is when we think the state of the system after taking all the actions will be better than the state before/in any other path

you have to keep all your actions "cleanly categorized". a lot of morality is confused because it tries to understand the action objectively, in all cases

# -------------------------------
# ORI Knowledge Rating Channel
# -------------------------------

I've just renamed `#research` channel to <#1344679873565823038> 

I want to try using this to share snippets of knowledge from across our network. 

1. Your goal when posting here is to deliver A's, or B's that you are trying to propagate further 
2. When reading, you want to rate each thing A/B/U (using emoji reactions). If you can, include OPTIONAL comments as a thread reply. 

(if you are rating A, try and "add onto" the knowledge to test if you really got it, or explain how you see it applying in your work. 

if rating B, describe where you first gained this insight if you can, or if you have any recommended reading for the person to further go down this direction)

3. Use the "ORI knowledge sharing format" (either by posting a screenshot, or just formatting it this way in discord). This is a "unit" of information for publishing within our network. Maybe we will have more genres of units in the future, but for now let's see how far this takes us

Right. So let’s think about a recording of a conversation. After that conversation, there is a point at which you can choose whether or not the conversation goes on the record. This ties into the reversibility. Once a conversation goes on the record, it’s on the record. This protocol sets a very specific set of rules that allow anyone involved in the recording of something the ability to intercept that before it hits the network and has to be deleted. At that point, the cost is too high. This allows us to have a social protocol for integrating ai workflows.

There is an infinite variety of meanings implied while talking/understanding something, and that wasteland of meanings we use every day, but there are three specific sets of meanings we use while taking and understanding something:

1) is the meaning of the crowd.

2)is the meaning of the wise, which is written in the dictionaries or defined by high ranking scientists et cetera

3) is the meaning of absolute.

So there are basically 3 types of "truths" we use, which are representations of the proximity to the absolute truth.

So depending of what exactly you expect out of the definition/ any definition, you get to use any of these three with quite the nuance, depending on who you are.

Computations (the cause and effect limit of our observational capacity) are part of a topological structure called the Ruliad - the space of all possible computations and computational systems. 

Structurally this is the ultimate  world of potential discourse, anything any observer like us can say/observe about exists.

To be a valid mathematical object that's probable (which we know is true as we already probe abstract and physical computational objects) the space must have closure. All computations in the limit ultimately complete - otherwise math, logic don't work, and we can't get physics!

This means it's one unified object. Ie connected in an infinite groupoid structure.

It is in this sense that Christ has a fallen human nature, yet also has a perfect human nature and has no sin. 

He participates in the same humanity that has sinned and also bore the consequences of sin despite being sinless. He has no disposition to sin, but was subjected to sin, lived among those who sinned, had a completed nature which encompassed the natures of those who sin, and ultimately in dying on the cross and rising from the dead fulfilled the promise of eternity for those who identify in him and choose his cross over their own sin.
Living a perfect life, dying a perfect death, rising up to a perfect throne.

Christ is the Son of man, downstream of Adam, but paradoxically also the Second Adam through being that firstborn into which we may be subsumed. He is the perfect man, for he is God incarnate. He is God incarnate, for he is the perfect man. The Son of man, and the Son of God. One person, in perfect union without mixing. He rose from the dead that we may believe. By his grace, we were given justifying faith.
I tend to become skeptical around truth want to look how it's framed because that's often more important. Plus there's like what, over a dozen different Truth theories?

Christ is the victim of original sin, having both been in a world affected by the consequences of sin and also directly on the receiving end of sin. This is all while he himself has no original sin. He had no disposition to sin despite the same temptations.

Right, because the overall goal for religion is to reconnect the divided.

Some people have only a spirit proximate to systematic truths and the evaluation of well-formed statements as true or false relative to its judgment. But Christian truth and falsehood are not syllogistic, at least not primarily. It is purely about whether it lives or dies.

The Christian religion not only seeks to connect (bring peace), but to divide (bring war). There are those who will love the way and the truth and the life, and there are those who will love rebellion and falsehood and death.
The dualism of Christianity is not between things in existence, but rather between existence and non-existence. This is where some evangelicals really mess up in their ontology of good and evil.

Nonexistence of course cannot exist in itself, but plenty of beings who exist desire that which cannot exist. And we could say that this makes for intrinsic friction between ultimate reality and proximate hypotheticals.

Fair, you can't have half a duality since it's holographic. Both sides need to be respected in whole. The garden of Eden is about this division and how we can't go back to being completely whole and have to deal with judgement.
The garden of Eden is about trusting our own discernment on what exists and what does not over the revelation of God.

My hypothetical might exist within its own substrate even as it is not a proper sign of its intended significance, which is reality. I think something will come true, but it will not come true. My fiction is a thing, but it does not correspond to the fact it is seeking to represent.
Ahh so that's the separation that we judge and don't just leave it up to God.

Something can be objectively true relative to a model, but the model itself could also not make meaningful predictions of the substrate to which it is referring. Yet people who are married to the model might then choose the model over the substrate in question. We see this a lot with ideologies.
This seems to perhaps tie into the faith aspect.

That's a great model to work from. So we can take a look at the options the employee faces. 

1. they can go to their boss and try to say they aren't being assigned useful work. This is likely to lead to a conflict because of the power dynamics at play. The employee feels threatened in their security if they have the conversation. But they also feel threatened if they don't. So they look for alternate options.

2. They can go above their boss - this has all sorts of downstream risks, because they have the potential to be seen as the person who doesn't respect authority, so the perception of that move is potentially bad for future moves by the employee.

3. They go to HR - that's a tactic that is almost universally bad, because their goal is a peaceful work environment, and the easiest person to get rid of is the person who is causing the issues

4. Find a different job/start a company/some other option - all tons of work. These have way more unknowns that the options above.

So we've gotten an entirely convoluted org structure that doesn't have a clear path to a solution. But what about a conscious system?

 A conscious system looks at the actual work being done. Is it filling in the gaps that it is expected to? Have things fallen off? It creates a system where everyone in the system has the power to look at the data and come to an agreement on what has changed and what hasn't. The system identifies the problem and raises it, escalating it when the people involved can't come to an agreement.

There isn't a winning play in the existing system because all of the complexity lives in each individual. They have to value their own planned future vs the planned future of the company, so they default to no-conflict no change structures because the only thing holding everything together is a little bit of stability granted by a salary. The only thing worse than a salary is no salary.

So, there's a bit of a paradox built in here between determinism and free will. You can view "your" hand raising from a 3rd perspective as just objective matter of fact while your experience was this was a choice. Then there's a variety of outcomes that happen based on God's will, or causality.

Think of it as ontology rather than as an operation within usual ontology. Free will is only that which is proximate to us, for only God is ultimately free. Anything else would dictate that there are sovereigns other than God.

I am not suggesting Molinist "middle knowledge", by the way. I am suggesting that God already chose literally every outcome, and the entirety of our free will is a matter of our own proximity even as only God's will is carried out. It is of course also true that our wills are contingent, though this does not stop the will from being genuine.

Only the body of Christ are the cells of God. All of creation moves according to the will of God, but not all creation is *of* God, in identity or will. Even the body of Satan can be said to act only by the will of God, but not one cell in the body of Satan is participating in the will of God.

I also reject the idea that the purpose of theological distinctives is necessarily for them to be visibly useful or to be immediately practical service. The purpose of theological distinctives is for them to be aligned to God in their spirit and their seeking. We should want to orient our thought and in fact the foundations of our thought toward God. This is not a matter of being cerebral but of founding everything on the premise that God is sovereign, because this is true before all else. The question in these cases is pretty much never about the actual difficulty or degree of abstraction involved, but rather about the perceived distance of this abstraction from how we naturally think. Yet, how we naturally think (without the grace of God) is totally depraved. We should want to discard that which we think has immediate value if there is something else which is built on the one true rock.

There is of course the doing part of faith which no amount of mere will reorientation without will expression can accomplish. But speaking is also a form of doing! Thinking is also a form of doing! Worshipping is itself a *sacrifice* of praise. To think with God placed at the very pinnacle, even if it is foreign or weird or uncomfortable or distant, is itself a form of devotion. You should want to worship God *even if* he decides to raze the world and save no one, because God is absolute and you should want what is absolute. Lick the biggest boot.

I often say that Mormon cosmology has some worldly wisdom to it given that there is an intimate sense of doing and creating. But this can only be seen as a proximate instrumental tool, part of the whole "wise as a serpent" thing which we do *after* we are called to faith and conformed to the ways of the Lord. If we are wise as serpents without continuing to ground ourselves in God above all else...well, Satan is the wisest serpent.

Moreover, the current context is that we are specifically talking about metaphysics, and this is not really the place to go into practical theological concerns for Christian living when not everyone here is even a Christian. Talking about the "practical" to someone without the same foundational realm of practice does not actually align wills to the true kingdom. It in fact further justifies conforming to the false kingdom.

Something surprising that I say to Christians is that Calvinist theology often is lacking in visible fruit in the more immediate realm, and this is something that *should* be rectified; every Christian is meant for eventual perfection (though not in this life) as God incarnate was perfect. But this is purely something that is meant for the ears of Christians who already are looking to God in all things. For everyone else, it suffices to say that Calvinist distinctives, insofar that they actually refer to what they are meant to refer to, are true, and we should abide in the way, the truth, and the life.
